item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal to be signed Sunday, Trump says
item.description: President Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened "immediately," once the memorandum of understanding is signed.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attack kills 1, injures 3 in southern Russia
item.description: Ukraine's General Staff said that its forces had hit an oil preparation and pumping station overnight in Russia's Volgograd region.

item.title: NBA star James Harden arrested in Houston for unlawful carrying of a weapon
item.description: NBA star James Harden was booked into jail and released on bond less than 2 hours later.

item.title: Police dress up as World Cup mascots to carry out drug sting
item.description: The officers in mascot costumes used a metal sledgehammer to break down a door to enter with colleagues.

item.title: Armed men kidnap respected security expert in gang-wracked Haiti
item.description: James Boyard is the cabinet director of Haiti's Defense Ministry and also serves as inspector general of Haiti's police.

item.title: "It rips my heart apart": U.S. military veteran calls on ICE to release wife
item.description: An American citizen who served in the Army and the Texas National Guard for roughly 20 years is desperately urging immigration officials to release his wife, who is facing deportation to Honduras.

item.title: At least 1 killed, 9 hospitalized in Texas mass shooting; suspect dead
item.description: The suspect, Victor Mata Villarreal, was wanted for attempted murder of a police officer after shooting at law enforcement during a vehicle chase earlier this week, officials said.

item.title: Equipment stolen from England's World Cup team recovered, BBC says
item.description: Footwear and soccer balls were among the items taken, the BBC reported, but the theft did not include anything "game-critical."

item.title: U.S. deports migrants from Afghanistan, Iran to Central African Republic
item.description: The Trump administration deported a group of roughly 20 migrants from Afghanistan, Iran and other nations to the Central African Republic, one of the world's poorest countries.

item.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center, court filing says
item.description: A federal appellate court denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center on Friday.

item.title: 6/13: Saturday Morning
item.description: The White House is readying for Sunday's UFC event as questions remain over peace negotiations with Iran. Plus, the U.S. dominated Paraguay 4-1 in the team's first World Cup game.

item.title: D.C.'s hottest food spot wants you to feel like you're at a dinner party
item.description: Jon Sybert and Jill Tyler opened up Rye Bunny in the Adams Morgan neighborhood of Washington, D.C., after closing their acclaimed Michelin-starred restaurant, Tail Up Goat, when its lease ended. "CBS Saturday Morning" goes inside one of the city's most popular spots for an experience akin to a dinner party.

item.title: A sportswriter and TV writer on what it means to be a sports fan
item.description: Sportswriter Joe Posnanski and television writer Michael Schur were brought together by their love of sports, so they decided to travel around the world to collect more stories of fandom. The two sit down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to discuss their book, "Big Fan: Two Friends, 82,490 Miles, and the Wild, Wonderful Sports We Love."

item.title: Full Interview: Jahmani "Hot Shot" Swanson
item.description: Jahmani Swanson, known as "Hot Shot" sat down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to talk about playing for the Harlem Globetrotters as the organization celebrates its 100th anniversary.

item.title: Metal grill brushes can cause serious injury. Here's how two best friends are changing that.
item.description: Earlier this year, more than 13 million grill brushes were recalled after multiple injuries were reported from wire bristles breaking off. Firefighter Scott Mobley and Anthony Tranchida partnered up to create a bristle-free brush that uses steam to clean your grill, and "CBS Saturday Morning" gets a first-hand look at how it works just in time for Father's Day. (Sponsored by Grill Rescue)

item.title: Harlem Globetrotters celebrate 100 wacky years
item.description: The Harlem Globetrotters have been entertaining crowds with their comedic athletic feats for a century. "CBS Saturday Morning" speaks with current team members on what it means to be part of the iconic squad.

item.title: Trump's changes to history at national parks must be undone, judge rules
item.description: President Trump issued an executive order in March 2025 ordering national parks to not display elements that "inappropriately disparage Americans past or living."

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Young the Giant performs "Mind Over Matter"
item.description: Young the Giant broke out in 2010 with hits like "My Body" and "Cough Syrup," which boast more than 400 million streams on Spotify. Their latest record, "Victory Garden," is out now. Here's Young the Giant performing "Mind Over Matter."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Young the Giant performs "Evergreen"
item.description: Young the Giant broke out in 2010 with hits like "My Body" and "Cough Syrup," which boast more than 400 million streams on Spotify. Their latest record, "Victory Garden," is out now. Here's Young the Giant performing "Evergreen."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Young the Giant performs "Different Kind of Love"
item.description: Young the Giant broke out in 2010 with hits like "My Body" and "Cough Syrup," which boast more than 400 million streams on Spotify. Their latest record, "Victory Garden," is out now. Here's Young the Giant performing "Different Kind of Love."

item.title: Former SpaceX welder becomes a millionaire after historic IPO
item.description: Juan Hernandez, a former SpaceX employee, owns 6,500 company shares. On the first day of public trading, his wealth ballooned by $1,046,175.

item.title: Meet the 103-year-old WWII veteran who defines the word "valor"
item.description: "CBS Saturday Morning" meets Richard "Dick" Nelms, a 103-year-old World War II veteran who still volunteers once a week at the Museum of Flight in Seattle.

item.title: Inside the UFC "claw" on the White House lawn
item.description: UFC Freedom 250 is set to take place on the White House lawn Sunday evening as part of a celebration for President Trump's 80th birthday. "CBS Saturday Morning" goes inside the setup.

item.title: Highlights from the most recent batch of UFO files
item.description: The Pentagon released a third batch of UFO files on Friday as the Trump administration continues its wave of disclosures.

item.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire with SpaceX IPO
item.description: Elon Musk became the world's first trillionaire on Friday after SpaceX executed the largest IPO in stock market history.

item.title: U.S. dominates Paraguay 4-1 in World Cup opener
item.description: The 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup games kicked off Thursday across the U.S., Mexico and Canada. On Friday, the U.S. Men's National Team beat Paraguay 4-1 in their opening match. Their next game is against Australia on June 19.

item.title: White House gears up for UFC event as uncertainty remains over an Iran deal
item.description: The White House is readying for a weekend of celebrations for President Trump's 80th birthday, including the UFC Freedom 250 event on Sunday night. Meanwhile, uncertainty remains over progress on a peace deal with Iran.

item.title: DOJ paves way for Paramount Skydance to buy Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: Paramount Skydance's $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery would not harm U.S. consumers or weaken competition, DOJ antitrust enforcers said.
